Title: A 3R-MYB transcription factor is involved in Methyl Jasmonate-Induced disease resistance in Agaricus bisporus and has implications for disease resistance in Arabidopsis

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/committed-step enzyme in the pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to host disease resistance against Pseudomonas tolaasii.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Agaricus bisporus fruiting body cap tissues adjacent to lesions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MeJA up-regulates AbPAL1 expression and PAL activity, promoting phenylpropanoid metabolite accumulation associated with enhanced disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; enzyme activity assay; inhibitor assay using AOA; metabolite profiling under MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by MeJA after P. tolaasii inoculation, especially from 36 h onward during storage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by MeJA after P. tolaasii inoculation, especially from 36 h onward during storage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μmol L−1 MeJA fumigation before P. tolaasii infection; storage at 4 °C; pathway inhibition by AOA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome sequencing mentioned for prior DEG mining, accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbPAL1 was markedly induced by MeJA during P. tolaasii infection in A. bisporus. 2. Its expression pattern paralleled increased PAL enzymatic activity. 3. Inhibition of PAL weakened MeJA-induced phenylpropanoid accumulation and disease resistance, supporting AbPAL1 involvement in the resistance-associated pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for enhancing phenylpropanoid-based disease resistance in edible mushrooms, though direct transgenic manipulation was not tested in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AbPAL2 was also measured but was not a major MeJA-responsive gene in the resistance context; AbPAL1 is the focal PAL gene in Results.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key pathway enzyme; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to host disease resistance against Pseudomonas tolaasii.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Agaricus bisporus fruiting body cap tissues adjacent to lesions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MeJA rapidly induces AbC4H1 expression and C4H activity, promoting phenylpropanoid accumulation linked to disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; enzyme activity assay; inhibitor assay using piperonylic acid; promoter activation/binding by AbMYB11
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced by MeJA, notably at 12–48 h after treatment under infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly induced by MeJA, notably at 12–48 h after treatment under infection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μmol L−1 MeJA fumigation before P. tolaasii infection; storage at 4 °C; pathway inhibition by PIP
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbC4H1 expression was rapidly increased by MeJA during infection. 2. C4H enzyme activity was elevated and inhibitor treatment weakened MeJA-induced resistance. 3. AbMYB11 directly bound and activated the AbC4H1 promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful in pathway engineering to reinforce phenylpropanoid-mediated defense when coordinated with upstream regulators such as AbMYB11.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AbC4H1 promoter contained MYB-binding motifs validated by EMSA and dual-luciferase assay.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream to midstream; branch-enabling activation step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key pathway enzyme; branch-control relevance implied
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to host disease resistance against Pseudomonas tolaasii.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Agaricus bisporus fruiting body cap tissues adjacent to lesions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MeJA induces Ab4CL1 expression and 4CL activity, supporting phenylpropanoid accumulation and disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; enzyme activity assay; inhibitor assay using MDCA; promoter activation/binding by AbMYB11
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapid MeJA response, especially at 0–12 h after treatment under infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapid MeJA response, especially at 0–12 h after treatment under infection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μmol L−1 MeJA fumigation before P. tolaasii infection; storage at 4 °C; pathway inhibition by MDCA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ab4CL1 was induced early by MeJA during P. tolaasii infection. 2. 4CL inhibition reduced phenylpropanoid accumulation and weakened MeJA-induced disease resistance. 3. AbMYB11 directly bound and activated the Ab4CL1 promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for strengthening defense-related phenylpropanoid flux, especially together with regulatory TF engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ab4CL2 was measured but showed little MeJA responsiveness; Ab4CL1 is the core MeJA-responsive 4CL gene in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 3R-MYB transcription factor with conserved N-terminal MYB domain/SANT-like repeats
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation via promoter binding to AACNG motif or AC-rich MYB-binding elements
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phenylpropanoid biosynthesis and pathogenesis-related defense gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AbPAL1, AbC4H1, Ab4CL1, and AbPR-like transcription
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Pivotal MeJA-inducible regulator linking pathogen response to phenylpropanoid metabolite accumulation and resistance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phenylpropanoid metabolites contribute to resistance against Pseudomonas tolaasii; flavonoids/phenolics/lignin support defense
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Agaricus bisporus fruiting body cap tissues; Arabidopsis leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AbMYB11 promotes phenylpropanoid accumulation and enhances resistance to P. tolaasii in A. bisporus and when overexpressed in Arabidopsis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization; qPCR; dual-luciferase reporter assay; EMSA; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; metabolite profiling; disease assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly and significantly induced by MeJA under P. tolaasii infection, highest within 36 h post-inoculation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In Arabidopsis overexpression lines, transgene expression was high in selected T3 lines; in A. bisporus it was rapidly induced by MeJA during infection
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA pretreatment/fumigation; P. tolaasii infection; postharvest storage in A. bisporus; Arabidopsis inoculation under continuous light at 22 °C
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Prior transcriptome sequencing identified AbMYB11 as a differentially expressed MYB gene; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Enhanced CaMV35S promoter (constitutive) used for Arabidopsis overexpression; CaMV35S promoter used in reporter normalization/control constructs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for breeding or engineering disease-resistant edible mushrooms and plants by enhancing phenylpropanoid-based defense; heterologous expression in Arabidopsis was effective.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbMYB11 is the only MYB among 13 identified MYB DEGs that was significantly induced early after P. tolaasii infection. 2. It localizes to the nucleus and is rapidly induced by MeJA during infection. 3. It directly binds MYB motifs in AbPAL1, AbC4H1, Ab4CL1, and AbPR-like promoters and activates their transcription. 4. Overexpression in Arabidopsis increases AtPAL1, AtC4H, At4CL1, and AtPR1 expression, elevates total phenolics and flavonoids, and enhances resistance to P. tolaasii.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AbMYB9, AbMYB10, and AbMYB12 were tested but down-regulated by MeJA and were not functionally characterized as core regulators. The paper emphasizes AbMYB11 as the central MYB controlling MeJA-induced defense.
